label: 156_dhs_homeland security_homeland_department homeland
label_human: Homeland security budget and fusion centers
description: The Department of Homeland Security receives significant funding for activities related to detecting and responding to terrorist threats. Debates surround the role and funding of state and local fusion centers, which share information on potential security risks but have faced criticism.

earliest_articles.title: H.R. 1417, Border Security Results Act of 2013
earliest_articles.excerpt: As ordered reported by the House Committee on Homeland Security on May 15, 2013 H.R. 1417 would require the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to measure the effectiveness of the department’s border security strategy at U.S. ports of entry and along U.S. borders (including maritime borders). newest_articles.title: H.R. 2259, National Strategy for School Security Act of 2025
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 2259 would require the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), in consultation with the Department of Education, to report to the Congress within one year of enactment on a strategy for improving security in elementary and secondary schools. Under the bill, the strategy would need to include a description of…

newest_articles.title: H.R. 2212, DHS Intelligence Rotational Assignment Program and Law Enforcement Support Act
newest_articles.excerpt: H.R. 2212 would require all agencies within the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) that conduct intelligence activities to participate in the Intelligence Community Civilian Joint Duty Program. That program allows intelligence analysts to work temporarily at different agencies within the intelligence community,…

newest_articles.title: S. 848, Reporting Efficiently to Proper Officials in Response to Terrorism Act of 2025
newest_articles.excerpt: S. 848 would require any federal agency that investigates an act of terrorism to submit an unclassified report to the Congress within one year of completing the investigation, and to make that report available to the public. The report would identify each act of terrorism as well as weaknesses in homeland security or…

central_articles.excerpt: The Proposed Homeland Security Budget for 2013 The Administration has proposed a budget of $69 billion for activities related to homeland security in 2013; 90 percent would be concentrated in the Departments of Homeland Security, Defense, Health and Human Services, and Justice. central_articles.title: Fusion Centers: What’s working and what isn’t
central_articles.excerpt: One of the more contentious debates in homeland security over the past several years has concerned fusion centers—state and local run organizations dedicated to information sharing and analysis. These centers, which are funded by state and local governments but also receive federal support, have raised concerns with…

central_articles.title: CBO Releases a Report on the Proposed Homeland Security Budget for 2013
central_articles.excerpt: Since the terrorist attacks of September 11, 2001, the federal government has spent more than half a trillion dollars on homeland security—that is, activities that detect, deter, protect against, and respond to terrorist acts occurring within the United States and its territories.
